description: Part C: QuestbookScreen in its full glory - parchment look, binding/spine, framed pages, dog-ears/shadows, open animation (scale tween), page-turn animation (scaleX flip), real data from SliceDirector
steps: Modify game/src/desktopMain/kotlin/game/QuestbookScreen.kt: Complete visual overhaul while keeping the same data flow (companion object vars for entries/markers/partyName/pressure). Replace flat colored rects with: (1) parchment gradient background using multiple overlapping SolidRects with graduated RGBA values to simulate aged paper, (2) dark binding/spine in the center (multiple narrow rects for 3D depth illusion), (3) framed pages with ornamental border rects (inner+outer frame), (4) dog-ear corner effect (small triangular overlay in top-right using angled SolidRect), (5) page edge shadows (thin dark rects on right side of left page, left side of right page).

Add open animation: Use KorGE tween API (korlibs.korge.tween.tween with korlibs.time Easing). On scene enter, the book container starts at scaleX=0.0, scaleY=0.8 and tweens to scaleX=1.0, scaleY=1.0 over 0.4s with easeOutBack. Wrap all book content in a Container that gets the tween applied.

Add page-turn animation: Track currentPage (left=0, right=1 to start, then pairs). On LEFT/RIGHT arrow key press, animate a 'page flip' by tweening an overlay rect's scaleX from 1.0 to 0.0 then 0.0 to 1.0 (simulating a page folding over) over 0.3s. Update content between the two half-tweens.

Content pages: Page 0 (left) = quest log entries from entries (show up to 8, truncated), Page 1 (right) = active assignments + party name + pressure. Additional pages for overflow entries (paginated). Use existing data from companion object vars.

Verify tween imports: korlibs.korge.tween.tween, korlibs.korge.tween.get (for property animation), korlibs.time.seconds for duration, korlibs.math.interpolation.Easing for easing curves. These are standard KorGE 6.0 APIs.
acceptance_criteria: QuestbookScreen renders parchment background with visible binding, frames, and dog-ears
Open animation uses tween API for scale transition
Page-turn responds to LEFT/RIGHT keys with scaleX flip animation
Real data from SliceDirector is displayed (entries, markers, partyName, pressure)
J/ESC still closes the screen and returns to WorldScene
findings: Easing.Companion.invoke({ name }, { t -> ... }) is the factory for custom easings in KorGE 6.0.0. Only LINEAR and SMOOTH are built-in. Tween time param accepts kotlin.time.Duration (400.milliseconds). V2 get operator from korlibs.korge.tween.get works with KMutableProperty0<Double> for scaleX/scaleY animation. Container used for grouping views (SolidRect is leaf). korlibs.math.interpolation.Easing is in korlibs-math-core transitive dep.
